prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
In the quiet hush of victory's sweet embrace,  
A sigh of relief to wander, a calm, softly-spaced.  
Hours woven into an intricate, entangled lace,  
Now unravel with delight, no more to chase.  

The endless journey of sketches and rewritten words  
Has crossed the horizon, silence from questions heard.  
Labor's heavy shadows cast away, no more deterred,  
A masterpiece stands proudly, its complexity neared.  

Hands still, tired yet exultant, in twilight's glow,  
A tapestry complete, bit by bit, stitch by stitch grow.  
The echo of finality fills the spaces, so low,  
Yet filled with the elation only creators know.  

In this newfound peace, heart dares to believe,  
Every challenge faced was worth the weave.
